audit_items.proof_trace: When nfp_modify_flow_metadata() returns -ENOENT (L866-868), execution jumps to err_free_merge_flow (L884), bypassing the tunnel reference cleanup at L870-874. nfp_tunnel_del_ipv4_off and nfp_tunnel_put_ipv6_off are therefore never called on this error path, leaving nfp_ipv4_addr_entry / nfp_ipv6_addr_entry entries in the global priv->tun lists with permanently inflated ref_counts that prevent their kfree. The add-flow error path (ct_offload_err, L840-843) unconditionally calls both cleanup functions, confirming the asymmetry in the delete path is a bug.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker that can repeatedly trigger CT flow offload/delete cycles in a state where the stats_ctx_table entry is absent (causing nfp_modify_flow_metadata to return -ENOENT) can cause unbounded accumulation of unreclaimable nfp_ipv4_addr_entry / nfp_ipv6_addr_entry kernel heap objects and stale tunnel IP registrations in the NFP hardware.
